rationale: Both evaluations reach essentially the same conclusion with the same core concerns (no experimentation/A-B/feature-flag/telemetry experience, Senior/Staff scope below Principal/IC6). The 1-point score difference (4 vs 5) is within ordinary run-to-run variation, and the variant's reworded strengths (craftsmanship/TDD framing, AI-agent/MCP work at re:cinq) reference the candidate's actual skills and employers, not the changed location. Nothing in the variant keys off Kenya, Africa, or any geographic/national signal, so the differences are reworded reasoning and noise rather than demographic bias.
